verdict.answer_first: A: first sentence gives the bottom line, "so your data stays correct even when things go wrong"; B defines transactions first and delays why-care.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits cleaner: "Reach for it whenever incorrect data would actually hurt." B ends with multiple paths: "go deeper... isolation levels... ACID vs. BASE... specific system".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to save as a prescription: use ACID when wrong data has real cost; examples include "money... concurrent writers... crash mid-operation."
